title: Good And Bad Luck. Translations. After Heine.
content:
Good luck is the gayest of all gay girls,
        Long in one place she will not stay;
    Back from your brow she strokes the curls,
        Kisses you quick and flies away.

    But Madame Bad Luck soberly comes
        And stays, - no fancy has she for flitting, -
    Snatches of true love-songs she hums,
        And sits by your bed, and brings her knitting.
meaning:
John Milton Hay's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Good And Bad Luck. Translations. After Heine."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
